Disagree. While the initial consensus was accurate regarding the exclusion of the superior oblique muscle under normal circumstances, the question asks for what a lesion specifically of the oculomotor nerve (CN III) affects, which includes all other extraocular muscles it innervates—superior rectus, medial rectus, and inferior oblique. If CN III were affected, none of these muscles would be spared; thus, the statement holds true universally within the context given. Hence, the correct answer remains **C**: the superior oblique, being separately innervated by CN IV, stands unaltered even after a lesion in CN III. Answer choice: C.

The key point is reiterating that although a lesion in CN III affects many muscles, the superior oblique is still excluded from this group precisely because it has alternative innervation.
